channel.title: Extraterrestrial Life on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Extraterrestrial Life on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Public opinion on extraterrestrial life
item.description:
Public opinion surveys indicate a widespread belief in the existence of intelligent alien civilizations, often exceeding scientific confidence levels. Studies show significant cross-national variation in belief, with some research highlighting a gap between public perception and expert consensus. Demographic factors, such as religious observance and age, correlate with differing beliefs, and psychological factors like exposure to UFO content also play a role. Public interest in extraterrestrial life is further reflected in search trends and the popularity of media related to speculative theories.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_opinion_on_extraterrestrial_life

item.title: NASA Documents Show Renewed Internal Planning on How to Announce Discovery of Extraterrestrial Life
item.description:
NASA has released documents detailing internal discussions about how the agency would communicate a confirmed discovery of extraterrestrial life. These documents include a June 2025 meeting invitation focused on developing a formal communications protocol. This planning builds upon earlier work, with a 2020 presentation outlining foundational considerations for such a scenario, emphasizing equitable information dissemination and managing public reaction. While the documents confirm ongoing planning, they do not indicate any discovery has occurred, and significant portions of the deliberative process remain redacted.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/nasa-documents-show-renewed-internal-planning-on-how-to-announce-discovery-of-extraterrestrial-life

item.title: Linda Moulton Howe
item.description:
Linda Moulton Howe is an American investigative journalist and documentary filmmaker who rose to prominence for her reporting on environmental issues and, later, for her work in the field of ufology. She gained significant attention in 1980 for producing the documentary A Strange Harvest, which explored cattle mutilation theories and alleged extraterrestrial involvement in government activities. Throughout her career, Howe has contributed to numerous television programs and media projects related to UFOs, crop circles, and alien abductions, while frequently appearing as a guest on platforms like Coast to Coast AM. While some supporters regard her as a leading figure in the investigation of unexplained phenomena, critics such as Joe Nickell have characterized her work as credulous and lacking in verified evidence.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linda_Moulton_Howe

item.title: UFO (2018 film)
item.description:
UFO (2018) is a science fiction film written and directed by Ryan Eslinger that follows a mathematics student, played by Alex Sharp, as he attempts to prove the reality of extraterrestrial encounters. Assisted by a professor portrayed by Gillian Anderson, the protagonist decodes complex signals to challenge official government cover-ups regarding a UAP sighting. The film offers a grounded, math-centric take on the UFO genre, though critical reception suggests it serves primarily as a modest nod to classic alien-encounter cinema. Sony Pictures released the project directly to video on September 4, 2018.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%282018_film%29

item.title: List of ufologists
item.description:
This Wikipedia article provides a categorized list of notable individuals identified as ufologists, defined as people who investigate whether unidentified flying objects are linked to extraterrestrial intelligence. The list is organized by country and features researchers, authors, and public figures involved in UFO studies, such as J. Allen Hynek, Jacques Vallée, and Stanton Friedman. The entry notes that the article relies heavily on primary sources and includes a wide range of individuals spanning from scientific researchers to public advocates and figures associated with various UFO-related claims.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ufologists

item.title: The Extraterrestrial UFO Hypothesis (ETH)
item.description:
The extraterrestrial hypothesis (ETH) proposes that some unidentified flying objects (UFOs) are physical spacecraft piloted by intelligent extraterrestrial beings. While historical speculation about alien visitors dates back centuries, the modern ETH gained traction in the late 1940s and 1950s, influenced by early science fiction and widespread UFO sightings. Despite some historical government and private studies suggesting extraterrestrial origins, the scientific community largely attributes UFO reports to misinterpretations, hoaxes, or common phenomena, with no definitive physical evidence of alien visitation found.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extraterrestrial_UFO_hypothesis

item.title: International UFO Museum and Research Center
item.description:
The International UFO Museum and Research Center is located in Roswell, New Mexico, and was established in 1991 as a nonprofit educational organization. It is largely focused on the 1947 Roswell incident and other UFO events. The museum features exhibits on UFO encounters and an extensive library of related materials. It also serves as a central point for the annual UFOfest held in Roswell.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_UFO_Museum_and_Research_Center

item.title: UFO
item.description:
The 2018 American science fiction film UFO stars Gillian Anderson and Alex Sharp. It follows a mathematics student who attempts to prove the existence of extraterrestrials after a UFO sighting causes electromagnetic interference. With the help of his professor, he deciphers a message containing the fine-structure constant and coordinates. The film was released directly to video on September 4, 2018.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_%282018_film%29

item.title: Xenoarchaeology
item.description:
Xenoarchaeology is a hypothetical field of study concerned with the material remains of extraterrestrial cultures, primarily existing in science fiction. It is a branch of xenology that aims to reconstruct and interpret past alien civilizations. The field is not currently practiced by mainstream archaeologists due to a lack of actual material to study. Xenoarchaeology is also referred to as astroarchaeology or exoarchaeology and encompasses related concepts like SETI and SETA.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xenoarchaeology

item.title: Ratanabá conspiracy theory
item.description:
Ratanabá is a conspiracy theory about a lost city in the Brazilian Amazon rainforest, allegedly built by extraterrestrial beings. The theory was initiated by Urandir Fernandes de Oliveira and spread through social media, becoming a viral meme. Despite being debunked by archaeologists and dismissed as pseudoscientific, the theory gained traction, even being promoted by former Culture Secretary Mário Frias. The spread of misinformation related to Ratanabá has also threatened historical heritage sites, leading to official repudiation.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ratanab%C3%A1

item.title: Istanbul UFO Museum
item.description:
The Istanbul UFO Museum, established in 2001 in Beyoğlu, Istanbul, Turkey, was the fourth international UFO museum. Founded by Haktan Akdoğan’s Sirius UFO Space Sciences Research Center, it housed photos, videos, reports, UFO-alien models, and a library on UFOs and extraterrestrial life. The museum reportedly had a mobile version that traveled around Istanbul in a truck in 2011. The Istanbul UFO Museum is no longer open.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Istanbul_UFO_Museum

item.title: Fiat Lux
item.description:
Fiat Lux is a neo-revelationist UFO religion founded in 1980 by Erika Bertschinger-Eicke, who was known as Uriella. Members of the group, primarily located in Germany’s Black Forest, believe in the imminent apocalypse and the arrival of alien ships to evacuate adherents to a new paradise called “Amora.” Uriella, who claimed to receive divine messages, died in 2019. The organization has been described as a cult and has an estimated following of thousands.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiat_Lux_%28UFO_religion%29

item.title: Flying Saucers from Outer Space
item.description:
Flying Saucers from Outer Space is a non-fiction book about UFOs written by Donald Keyhoe and published on October 5, 1953. The book was followed by The Flying Saucer Conspiracy in 1955 and preceded by The Flying Saucers Are Real in 1950. In 1956, a science-fiction film titled Earth vs. the Flying Saucers was made, suggested by Keyhoe’s book.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flying_Saucers_from_Outer_Space
